dict.string: Finder folder actions: flatten nested files to the root, delete empty folders, or group files into folders by extension.
Remove all folders and deliver the files to the root.

#!/bin/zsh
# Use AppleScript to get the current Finder directory DIR=$(osascript -e 'tell application "Finder" to if exists window 1 then POSIX path of (target of window 1 as alias)')
# Check if DIR is not empty if [[ -n "$DIR" ]]; then # Move nested files (at least one folder deep) to the top-level directory, # with duplicate handling. mindepth 2 leaves loose root files untouched. find "$DIR" -mindepth 2 -type f | while IFS= read -r file; do base=$(basename "$file") target="$DIR/$base" # If the target file already exists, append a number to the filename if [[ -e "$target" ]]; then ext="${base##*.}" name="${base%.*}" count=1 while [[ -e "$DIR/${name}_$count.$ext" ]]; do ((count++)) done target="$DIR/${name}_$count.$ext" fi mv "$file" "$target" done
# Repeatedly attempt to remove empty directories while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do rmdir "$dir" 2>/dev/null done < <(find "$DIR" -type d -mindepth 1 -print0 | sort -zr) else echo "No Finder window detected." fi
Deletes all empty folders and subfolders in the frontmost Finder window

on killEmpty(fol) tell application "Finder" repeat with f in (get fol's folders) my killEmpty(f) end repeat if (count items of fol) is 0 then delete fol delay 0.1 end tell end killEmpty tell application "Finder" repeat with f in (get folders of window 1) my killEmpty(f) end repeat end tell
Creates folders for each file type and sorts accordingly.

#!/bin/zsh
# Use AppleScript to get the current Finder directory DIR=$(osascript -e 'tell application "Finder" to if exists window 1 then POSIX path of (target of window 1 as alias)')
# Check if DIR is not empty if [[ -n "$DIR" ]]; then # Find all files and group them by extension, ignoring dot files find "$DIR" -type f -mindepth 1 | while IFS= read -r file; do filename=$(basename "$file")
# Skip files starting with a dot if [[ "$filename" == .* ]]; then continue fi
# Skip files with no extension (leave them where they are) if [[ "$filename" != *.* ]]; then continue fi
ext="${filename##*.}" ext="${ext:l}" # lowercase so .JPG and .jpg share one folder ext_dir="$DIR/$ext"
# Create a directory for the extension if it doesn't exist mkdir -p "$ext_dir"
# Move the file into the corresponding extension directory mv -n "$file" "$ext_dir/" done
# Optionally, remove any empty directories left over while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do rmdir "$dir" 2>/dev/null done < <(find "$DIR" -type d -mindepth 1 -print0 | sort -zr) else echo "No Finder window detected." fi

Finder folder actions. Run a keyword against the frontmost Finder window:
• unfold — Flatten Folder Hierarchy: move every nested file up to the top level (duplicates get a numeric suffix), then remove the emptied subfolders. • defold — Delete Empty Folders: recursively remove empty folders and subfolders. • tidy — Group Files by Extension: sort files into folders named for their (lowercased) extension.
All three act on the folder open in the frontmost Finder window.
